text: ⚡️ 🇬🇧 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has urged Iran and the United States to “find a way through” after talks in Pakistan ended without a deal. Downing Street said Starmer, in a conversation with the Sultan of Oman, stressed that a continuation of the ceasefire is “vital” and that all parties must avoid further escalation. Health Secretary Wes Streeting called the failed talks “disappointing” and defended Britain’s decision not to join the conflict. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Two U.S. warships entered the Strait of Hormuz yesterday but turned back after an Iranian drone appeared overhead. This was reported by The New York Times, citing sources familiar with the operation. The ships were expected to demonstrate that the passage was secure—potentially the first step toward a mine-clearing mission. Instead, they withdrew after an Iranian reconnaissance drone showed up. The drone was reportedly shot down. According to sources, the two U.S. Navy vessels moved out of the Gulf of Oman, entered the strait, and then reversed course. While U.S. officials said the drone posed no direct threat, the Navy chose not to proceed under active Iranian surveillance. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Ahead of Tuesday’s talks between Israeli and Lebanese representatives, the Maronite Patriarch, Beshara Boutros al‑Rai, called for peace in his Easter sermon. “Enough wars, enough attacks, enough turning Lebanon into an arena for settling scores,” he said. “Peace is not imposed by violence or force, but built through dialogue and negotiation. Peace is not born from victory, but from a true will that respects human life and preserves dignity.” @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇶 Al-Araby Al-Jadeed : Iraq’s political scene has shifted to a new crisis over the premiership after the election of President Nizar Amidi. The Coordination Framework is deeply divided, with growing momentum to exclude its current nominee Nouri al‑Maliki. His State of Law bloc boycotted the presidential vote alongside the Kurdistan Democratic Party. The constitution allows 15 days to name a prime minister. Some factions are pushing for a second term for the incumbent Mohammed Shia al‑Sudani. Maliki’s chances are weakening; a new candidate may be chosen by majority vote even if he boycotts. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Al-Akhbar in a analysis piece argues that the United States cannot serve as a neutral mediator in the Lebanon‑Israel conflict because it is a direct party to the war – militarily, politically and financially supporting Israel. Historical examples including Camp David (1978), Oslo (1993) and the 2024 ceasefire mechanism show US‑led mediation has produced fragile, unbalanced arrangements that favour Israel. Accepting a biased mediator, the article concludes, does not end the war but transfers it to an uneven negotiating table, weakening the weaker party - in this case Lebanon. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Channel 15 in Hebrew: The Chief of Staff has directed the Israeli army to an immediate state of alert in preparation for war with Iran. A decision on resuming the bombing has not yet been made.

⚡️ 🇵🇸 🇪🇬 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 A Hamas delegation is holding talks in Cairo with Egyptian mediators and Nikolay Mladenov, the senior representative of the US‑backed Peace Council, to discuss the second phase of the Gaza ceasefire – including the disarmament of Hamas and the entry of a technocratic committee to govern the Strip. Hamas insists on halting Israeli violations first and has rejected Mladenov’s claims about aid volumes. The Peace Council has received less than $1 billion of the $17 billion pledged for Gaza, with only the U.S., UAE and Morocco contributing so far, hindering reconstruction. The national committee for Gaza remains stuck in Cairo with no real power and domestic legitimacy. Israel is sceptical that Hamas will agree to give up its weapons and has already started Exigency plans to reopen the Gaza front if talks do fail. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇹🇷 🇮🇱 Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan threatened that Turkey could carry out a military intervention against Israel similar to its operations in Libya and Nagorno‑Karabakh. “Just as we entered Libya and Karabakh, we can enter Israel. There is no reason not to do it,” he said during a speech. Erdogan accused Israel of killing hundreds of innocent Lebanese during the ceasefire and called Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu “blinded by blood and hatred." @wfwitness

Iran’s lead negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, said the…" ⚡️ 🇸🇦 🇮🇷 Saudi Arabia has restored its East‑West oil pipeline to full capacity after attacks last week that caused a loss of approximately 700,000 barrels per day. The 1,200‑kilometre pipeline bypasses the Strait of Hormuz. Pumping capacity has returned to about 7 million barrels per day, with Manifa field producing 300,000 barrels per day. Work continues to restore full production at the Khurais field. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷🇵🇰🇱🇧 US‑Iran talks in Islamabad ended without a breakthrough after nine hours. Iran presented reasonable initiatives but excessive U.S. demands prevented a framework, Per Tasnim. The Key obstacles: Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. refused…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 CNN : U.S. Vice President JD Vance said no agreement was reached with Iran after 21 hours of marathon talks in Islamabad, with the US giving its “final and best offer." Iran’s lead negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, said the US failed to gain the trust of the Iranian delegation. Primary sticking points included Iran’s refusal to abandon its nuclear programme. The future of the two‑week ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz are now in doubt. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf: "The United States failed to gain Iran’s trust in response to our initiatives." ➡️ Before the negotiations, I emphasized that we have the necessary goodwill and determination, but due to the experiences of the previous two wars, we do not trust the other side. ➡️ My colleagues in the Iranian delegation at “Minab 168” presented forward-looking initiatives, but in the end, the other side failed to earn the trust of the Iranian delegation in this round of talks. ➡️ The United States understood our logic and principles, and now it is time for it to decide whether it can gain our trust or not. ➡️ At all times, we consider “power-based diplomacy” as another approach alongside military struggle to secure the rights of the Iranian people, and we will not cease for a moment our efforts to consolidate the achievements of 40 days of Iran’s national defense. ➡️ I appreciate the efforts of the friendly and brotherly country of Pakistan in facilitating this negotiation process, and I send my greetings to the people of Pakistan. ➡️ Iran is one body with 90 million souls. I thank the entire heroic nation of Iran, who—following the guidance of the Supreme Leader—supported their compatriots by taking to the streets and sending their prayers with us. I also commend my colleagues for their efforts in these intensive 21-hour negotiations. ➡️ Long live and prosper beloved Iran! 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Argentina’s debt to the IMF has climbed about 36% over the past year to roughly $57 billion, largely due to a new $20 billion. The new disbursements have increased Argentina’s total exposure, making it the IMF’s largest borrower.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇱🇧🇺🇳🇺🇸 Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has decided to delay his trip to the United Nations and the United States in order to continue managing the government’s work from Beirut amid the current internal situation. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇸🇾 MTV: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has postponed his U.S. visit after internal advice warned of a potential trap involving an alleged secret meeting with an Israeli envoy. He may face pressure to meet Israeli representatives, risking domestic backlash. The visit has not been cancelled. Lebanese officials confirmed that Lebanon has separated its issue from Iran’s in preliminary talks with the U.S. and Israel, insisting on a ceasefire before negotiations. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 🇱🇧 US‑Iran talks in Islamabad ended without a breakthrough after nine hours. Iran presented reasonable initiatives but excessive U.S. demands prevented a framework, Per Tasnim. The Key obstacles: Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. refused to include Lebanon in any deal with Iran, wanting to hand the issue entirely to Israel. On Hormuz, Washington sought to open it now without a future Iranian role. Iran insists no change to the strait’s status unless a reasonable agreement is reached. The U.S. proposed that enriched uranium be removed from Iranian soil – a red line for Tehran. Another round may take place within 7‑10 days with a different U.S. delegation level, Per Al-Araby Al-Jadeed. Israel believes the ceasefire may be extended to allow continued contacts, Per Kan. @wfwitness

Iran's Ghalibaf on failed negotiations with the U.S. in Pakistan: We have the necessary goodwill and determination, but due to the experience of two previous wars, we do not trust the other side. The other side ultimately failed to gain the trust of the Iranian delegation in this round of negotiations America has understood our logic and principles. Now it is time for it to decide whether it can earn our trust or not. We consider ‘power diplomacy’ another method alongside military struggle to secure the rights of the Iranian nation.

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf said that after 21 hours of intense talks in Islamabad, the U.S. failed to gain the trust of the Iranian delegation. “America understood our logic and principles. Now it is time for them to decide whether they can earn our trust,” he stated. Ghalibaf noted that Iran had good faith and will, but due to the experience of two previous wars, there is no trust in the US. He described “diplomacy of power” as another path alongside military struggle to secure Iran’s rights. He thanked Pakistan for facilitating the talks and praised the Iranian people for their support. @wfwitness
